codeReview.summary: The worker made 5 targeted fixes to 6 files addressing all Python 3.12/3.13/3.14 compatibility issues. All fixes are technically correct and well-scoped. The implementation correctly handles: (1) the typing._Immutable removal in 3.12 using a version guard, (2) the scipy.linalg.kron removal via numpy.kron substitution, (3) the nilearn private API relocation via try/except compatibility shims, and (4) invalid escape sequences in the Lark grammar string. I independently verified that `import neurolang` succeeds on Python 3.12, 3.13, and 3.14; the 40 core expression/grammar tests all pass; no distutils imports remain; and the Lark parser correctly handles the updated regex. Note: commitId in handoff (a7972725) refers to the library-notes commit; the actual source code changes are in commit 2bb80000 (the parent).

issues.description: The IDENTIFIER_REGEXP change removes the \/ escape (replacing with \\/) and the \. escape (replacing with just .). These are semantically equivalent in Python regex character classes, so the fix is functionally correct. However, the Lark grammar string now contains /[0-9a-zA-Z\\/#%._:-]+/ which, when the Python string is evaluated, produces the Lark regex pattern /[0-9a-zA-Z\/#%._:-]+/. The \\ (double backslash) in the Python string becomes a single \ in the Lark regex, which is redundant-but-harmless for the / character in a character class. This is cosmetically sub-optimal (a bare / would be cleaner) but is functionally correct and not a bug.

The handoff reports commitId as 'a7972725' which is only the library-notes commit. The actual source code fixes are in commit '2bb80000' (the parent commit). This is a minor discrepancy in the handoff metadata — the worker may have intended to reference the final HEAD commit after both commits. Both commits are on the migrate-pyproject-uv branch and belong to this feature's work.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The migration-worker skill step 6 says to create separate venvs with 'uv venv --python 3.12 .venv-312' for cross-version testing. However, the worker's handoff skillFeedback notes that 'uv run --python 3.X creates a new .venv (overwriting the existing one), and that workers should use separate named venvs (.venv-313, .venv-314) for cross-version testing instead.' The skill procedure is technically correct (it says to use uv venv to create named venvs) but the procedure is phrased as optional/implied context, not as a warning about the venv overwrite behavior. Adding an explicit warning note would prevent future workers from accidentally overwriting their default .venv.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges[0]: 'The migration-worker skill could mention that uv run --python 3.X creates a new .venv (overwriting the existing one), and that workers should use separate named venvs (.venv-313, .venv-314) for cross-version testing instead.' — The worker named their venvs .venv-313 and .venv-314 rather than the skill's suggested .venv-312 naming convention, indicating some confusion about the exact naming.

summary: PASS. All 5 compatibility fixes are correctly implemented and verified. No distutils imports remain (VAL-COMPAT-001 fulfilled). typing_inspect works correctly on Python 3.12 (VAL-COMPAT-002 fulfilled). `import neurolang` succeeds on Python 3.12, 3.13, and 3.14. The 40 core tests pass. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) a cosmetically redundant double-backslash in the Lark grammar regex (functionally correct), and (2) the handoff commitId points to the library-notes commit rather than the source code fix commit.
